feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to update a Role's model level privacy restrictions in SAC using an API
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we are looking into automating SAC Security. 

SAC tool provides import of users, so we can accommodate the new users, or updates to existing users to certain extent. 

Similarly, if we get import of teams updates, that would be great. Is there a way we can do this through an API, using a file upload, etc.

Also, if Role's model level privacy restrictions in SAC can be updated using an API, then all the aspects can be fully automated. 

Thanks & Best Regards,

SP

entry.title: SAC Dashboard 'Variance %' Threshold values color should appear on 'Actual' Value
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement of Bar chart with Actual,  plan and variance %.

In the SAC  Dashboard, the 'Variance %' threshold color is intended to appear on the 'Actual' value, not on the variance itself.

Variance in threshold values which changes the color dynamically based on % values.

GE = 0  'Green'

 0 to -0.2 ' yellow'

Less then -0.2 ' Red's

As shown in the below Pic.  'Ava Power Variance' threshold color should appear same color on 'Availability Power - AYTD%'.

Please suggest how we can change the 'Actual' color dynamically based on  "Variance %'  Threshold Colors.  

Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Analytics cloud planning - Data action issue
entry.content:
Hi, I’m facing an issue with a Data Action where users select a start and end time period to perform employee transfers. However, the Data Action is currently running even on closed periods—meaning the actual periods—where I don’t want the new data to appear. The problem is that I’m scoping the time dimension based on user-selected parameters rather than using version properties, so the Data Action affects both actual and planning periods indiscriminately.

To solve this, I need a way to restrict the Data Action so that it only updates data for planning periods within the user’s selected range and completely excludes any actual (closed) periods, even if they fall between the start and end dates. For example, if the user selects January to June 2025, but January to March 2025 are actual periods, the Data Action should only process April to June 2025, leaving the actual periods untouched.  i have locked data at model level but i dont want to show the user any data movements on actual periods. Can anyone please help.

@N1kh1l 

Regards,

Lakshmi

entry.title: OTP based login in SAC dashboard
entry.content:
Hii community,

I am trying to create an OTP based login in my SAC dashboard where i will be able to see my SAC dashboards based on provided OTP and after clicking submit button i will be navigated to my dashboards with proper data filtered based on the OTP used.  Does anyone have any idea on how to achieve this.

entry.title: Unable to get the desired data from REST API to SAC
entry.content:
I have a REST API in SuccessFactors and I need to consume its data in a SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). I have successfully created a import connection, but when trying to import the data, I am unable to see the desired data (employee compensation). I can see the data in POSTMAN. 

Please help me to fix this issue.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud: How do I build dynamic “Current-Year vs. Prior-Year” KPIs
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m building a dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud – Analytics Designer where the user enters a year (e.g. 2025) and the app should immediately show:

  • Bruttogewicht Lieferung (current year)
  • Bruttogewicht Lieferung (prior year)
  • Bruttogewicht Lieferung (prior-prior year)
  • an automatic %-deviation between current year and prior year (Numerical Point widget)

It means, that the user can input the current year in the input field, our script calculates the prior and prior-prior year and gives us the results. 

What I already have:
An InputField for the year

  • A Numeric Point (Chart_4) with two feeds
  • ValueAxis → Primary value
  • ValueAxis2 → Comparison value

  •  

  • Working script that filters all charts to the three selected years:
ds.setDimensionFilter("BDATU_JAHR", { values: [year, year-1, year-2] });

Script that removes all measures from the Numeric Point and adds two IDs:

chart.addMeasure("ValueAxis", "BG_2025"); chart.addMeasure("ValueAxis2", "BG_2024");

–> works fine if the Restricted Measures BG_2025, BG_2024, … already exist.

Pain point:
I don’t want to create a new Restricted Measure every single year manually. I had the following idea: 

Have three reusable Restricted Measures (current year, prior, prior-prior). Change their year filter (value in the dimension, used for the restriction) dynamically in script or swap them in/out without maintaining dozens of hard-coded IDs? My question is: Can I simply create 3 restricted measures: current year, prior year and prior-prior year and just change the dimension-value (2025, 2024, 2023 and so on), using scripting? 

My code, that I have created so far looks like that. Here I just works with different restricted measures for each year, that I created manually. I do not want 2025, 2024, 2023, but rather: : current year, prior year and prior-prior year, which 'filters' (years) will be changed dynamically.

/* ───────── 1. Wert aus dem Eingabefeld holen ───────── */
var actualYear = InputField_1.getValue();
var current_date = new Date().getFullYear();

// Zeichenfolge in das Datum konvertieren
var yearNum = ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(actualYear);

if (yearNum >= 1931 && yearNum <= current_date) {
	// Aktuelles, -Vor und -Vorvorjahr berechnen
	VariableAktualYear = yearNum;
	VariablePastYear = yearNum - 1;
	VariablePastPastYear = yearNum - 2;	
	
	var msg_1 = VariableAktualYear.toString();                           
	var msg_2 = VariablePastYear.toString();
	var msg_3 = VariablePastPastYear.toString();
	
	/* Daten quelle des Charts holen und den modifizieren */
	var ds = Chart_1.getDataSource();

	ds.setDimensionFilter("BDATU_JAHR", {values: [msg_1, msg_2, msg_3]});

	
	/* ───────── 2. Measures tauschen ───────── */
	//var measAct  = "BG_" + msg_1;       
	//var measPrev = "BG_" + msg_2;
	//Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, measAct);
	//Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, measPrev);
 
	// 23735103-3540-4518-3856-333700453218 - Jahr 2025
	// 42543347-2523-4283-3882-314686436141 - jahr 2024 
	// 32937114-7011-4110-3663-482995517414 - Jahr 2023
	// 29229304-1622-4153-3546-241342908683 - Jahr 2022
	
	var idMap = {
	  2025 : "23735103-3540-4518-3856-333700453218",
	  2024 : "42543347-2523-4283-3882-314686436141",
	  2023 : "32937114-7011-4110-3663-482995517414",
	  2022 : "29229304-1622-4153-3546-241342908683"
	};
	
	var measAct = idMap[msg_1];
	var measPrev = idMap[msg_2];
		

	var chart = Chart_4;
	var measures = Chart_4.getMeasures(Feed.ValueAxis);
	
	for (var i = 0; i < measures.length; i++) {
		chart.removeMeasure(measures[i], Feed.ValueAxis);
		Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Removed: ' + measures[i]);
	}
	
	Chart_4.addMeasure(measAct, Feed.ValueAxis);
	Chart_4.addMeasure(measPrev, Feed.ValueAxis);

	
	/* ───────── 2. Measures tauschen ───────── */

	
	// Check
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Aktuelles Jahr: '+ msg_1);
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Vorjahr: '+ msg_2);
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Info, 'Vorvorjahr: '+ msg_3);
	
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success,
        'Jahr ' + actualYear + ' übernommen.');

} else {
	var msg_4 = current_date.toString();
	Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error, 'Bitte eine vierstellige Jahreszahl (1931–' + msg_4 + ')');

I hope, you could help me with this issue.

entry.title: [SAC] - How to set the Story filter's active members?
entry.content:
As far as I know, there is a method called `setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy` that can control the members of a story filter. However, the effect I want is that when we enter a SAC page, the number of members in a story filter remains unchanged, but the selected values change. It seems to me that this cannot be achieved through scripting. Does anyone have any ideas?

Example:
By default, all options in the story filter (Fiscal Year Period) are selected:

When we enter a SAC page, the script can set it to look like the image below, with only 2009002 and 2010002 selected:

The above is the result I hope to achieve.

However, I found that only the `setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy` method can control changes to the Story Filter, and the result of this change is as shown below. But this result is not what I want because the other members are missing:

So my questions are:
1. Besides `setDimensionFilterWithHierarchy`, are there other methods to set the values of a story filter?
2. Is there any other way to achieve the effect I want?

entry.title: Unsupported and won't be available: No Linked Dimension is use for Blended Visualization
entry.content:
Is there a specific reason as to why this specific functionality won't be available in ODE? 

We have several dashboards still running in classic just because we can visualize unlinked dimensions in blended models.

Among the many developments done in ODE, this is a serious step back.

Is there a workaround when working with SAP BW live models?

entry.title: Variance to previous year on a year-to-date line chart compares current month fully not partially
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I got some revenue data on daily level that I show monthly aggregated in a line chart filtered on Year-to-date.

I enabled varance to see difference / Year-over-Year comparison. Working fine in general except for the current month.

There the variance is comparing to "complete month" of the previous year instead of the to-date time intervall of that month.  My expectation would be that when running story today the variance would compare 06/01/2025 ... 06/17/2025   to 06/01/2024 ... 06/17/2024 time intervals for the current month.

Do I do something wrong? 

Thanks and best regards
Axel

entry.title: How to add a max aggregation calculation for previous year in a SAP Analytics Cloud Story?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP Analytics Cloud at the story level, how can you create a variable that captures the maximum monthly value of a numeric field for the year preceding the one selected in a page filter (i.e., that captures the highest monthly value from the prior year)?

I've already created the variable to calculate the current year's maximum monthly value, but I've been unable to build the equivalent for the prior year. The requirement is to display both the current year's and the previous year's maximum monthly values simultaneously.

The story has:

  • Input control for years (single selection) based on budat
  • Input control for months (multiple selection) based on budat
  • Analytic import model consuming from s4 onpremise via cds view

I've tried the following alternatives without success:

Alternative 1: Restricted Measure for the Prior Year based on the MAX Aggregation of the current year:

1) Create a restricted measure for the prior year based on the current year's MAX aggregation: Defined a variable to capture the maximum value of the numeric field (acdoca.hsl) using Aggregation → MAX. This didn't return the correct value for the current year—likely due to the dataset's daily granularity.

2) Workaround: Create a alculated dimension for months. Added a dimension called vMonths to represent the month of each record.

This returns all months across every year in the dataset:

3) Recreate the “current year max” variable using vMonths: Defined a new variable to get the MAX of acdoca.hsl, scoped to the vMonths dimension. This returns the expected result when the page filter selects a single year, so it correctly serves as the “current year max” value.

4) Attempt to derive the prior‑year max from the current‑year variable: Created a restricted measure atop the “current year max” variable, again scoped to vMonths and set to Select by Member → All Members. This, however, still returns the current year's maximum rather than the prior year's.

5) Test using the budat date field: Tried setting budat values via various NAVIGATION options—but all failed.

6) Test using the budat field via SELECTION: Although the variable runs, it never returns the correct prior‑year max value.

Alternative 2: Calculated Measure Using the MAX() Function.

 1) This correctly returns the sum of all monthly values from the preceding year.

2) Apply the MAX() function in a calculated measure: Use a calculated measure with the MAX() function. Instead of yielding the highest single‐month value, it still returns the full annual total for hsl.

Alternative 3: “Overall HSL MAX”

1) Define the overall‑max aggregation variable: Create a variable (hsl_max) that captures the MAX of acdoca.hsl, scoped by the budat date field.

2) Attempt to derive the prior‑year max via a restricted measure: Apply a restricted‑measure filter on hsl_max for the year before the selected one. This approach fails to return the previous year's maximum; it still yields the current selection's max value.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Error with Add Member functionality in SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to add member by right clicking on the dimension header for existing member. its giving error as " Member is already booked".  Unbooked is switched off for the all the dimensions in the table. Can anyone please help on this.

    202501202502202503
CostcenterBUCityROle   
Trying to add same CC1 hereUnassignedPechinoTA   
CC1ProductsMilanoTL111

@N1kh1l

Regards,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Sales &and Profitability IFP Calculating Sales Deductions
entry.content:
Hello All,

I hope you're doing well.

I'm currently working on creating a customized Sales & Profitability module by replicating certain dimensions and measures from the standard Integrated Financial Planning (IFP) content into a dummy planning model for demo purposes.

While calculating Net Sales using the formula:
Net Sales = Gross Revenue – Sales Deductions,
I'm facing a challenge specific to the textile industry scenario I'm planning for.

The issue lies in the commission component of Sales Deductions, which varies by customer and product (Dimensions). For example, Customer A may have a 5% commission rate, while Customer B may have 6%. This variability impacts the net sales calculation.

Could anyone please guide me on how to incorporate customer-specific commission rates into the planning model to accurately calculate Net Sales per customer? do I need to create a separate model for that, data blending capabilities, or be able to incorporate that logic in the data action of main model instead of creating another model

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Can I create a dimension with latitude and longitude information in SAC?
entry.content: I have a model that has latitude and longitude based on zip code, how do I create a dimension "Region" from it? I can see the region on Maps , but I would like to create a dimension.

entry.title: Issue with Fiscal Week Configuration in SAC Planning Model
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm working on creating a Date dimension in a planning model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

I've successfully configured the fiscal year to start in May and to be labeled by the year in which it ends.

However, I need the fiscal week to start from the first Thursday of each month. Currently, the dropdown only allows selecting Saturday, Sunday, or Monday as the week start day.

Could anyone advise on how to achieve this or suggest a workaround?

Thanks in advance!

SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Integrated Business Planning for demand RISE with SAP

entry.title: How to Check View Counts for AM in sac add-in for excel
entry.content:
Hello.

User is looking up the AM created in datasphere through sac add-in for excel.

Is there an administrator menu where I can see how much each AM has been viewed in sac add-in for excel?

entry.title: Excel Trend function in SAC
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I wanted to achieve one functionality in SAC which is standard functionality in excel. I have the below data for 12 months:

 Jan FebMarAprMayJunJulAugSepOctNovDec
Account 191316181417141110141216

Using the Trend function, it normalizes the data or makes it linear as below:

 Jan FebMarAprMayJunJulAugSepOctNovDec
Account 171616151515141414131312

Is there any functionality in SAC to achieve this? Maybe using advanced script, we can do this calculation, but formula used behind the trend function is not straight forward.

Thanks & Regards,

Pranav Vyas

entry.title: Sales &and Profitability IFP Calculating Sales Deductions (Rebate Calculation)
entry.content:
m currently working on creating a customized Sales & Profitability module by replicating certain dimensions and measures from the standard Integrated Financial Planning (IFP) content into a dummy planning model for demo purposes.

While calculating Net Sales using the formula:
Net Sales = Gross Revenue – Sales Deductions,
I'm facing a challenge specific to the textile industry scenario I'm planning for.

The issue lies in the Rebate component of Sales Deductions, which varies by customer and product (Dimensions). or example, Customer A has products X, Y, and Z, each with different rebate percentages—2%, 3%, and 4% respectively. How can we calculate these rebates and incorporate them into sales deductions for the purpose of computing net sales of export products?

Could anyone please guide me on how to incorporate customer & product-specific rebate rates into the planning model to accurately calculate Net Sales per customer?

entry.title: Adding Custom Tooltips or Popups for Comments/Links in Table Cells in SAP Analytics Cloud Story
entry.content:
Hey everyone!

I want to allow users to enter information (like a comment, explanation, or external link) that will not be visible in the main table, but instead will only appear:

  • When the user actively clicks a specific button

  • Or, when clicking on a specific cell or icon within the cell

This would ideally open a popup or floating window that displays the hidden content — like a comment or hyperlink — without cluttering the table itself.

My question:

Is there a way in SAP Analytics Cloud to implement such a mechanism?
Either through scripting, pop-up logic on onSelect events, or a custom widget?
I'd love to hear how others may have handled similar needs for clean tables with context hidden behind user interaction.

Thanks in advance for any tips, examples, or directions!

Best regards,
Elad Tayar

entry.title: Restrict Data Point Comments visibility to individual users in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
After enabling Allow Data Point Comments on a table in my SAP Analytics Cloud Story, I would like to know if is there a way to restrict the visibility of comments so that they are personal per user and not visible to all other users interacting with the same story/table?

If yes – I’d really appreciate some guidance on how to implement this.
If not – are there any alternative solutions to allow users to add private notes or comments on specific data cells?

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Content Material
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I’m currently preparing several presentations about the SAC and was wondering if anyone has any good recommendations for informative materials I could use.

I’d really appreciate any helpful sources you can share.

Looking forward to your suggestions!

Kind regards,
Ismail
